skill.name: patterns
skill.description: Loads project code patterns and abstraction guidance so implementation work follows existing conventions.
skill.summary: Use project pattern guidance when designing features, components, APIs, state handling, and error handling.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: The three high static findings are false positives. The cited lines contain ordinary documentation about code patterns and API design, with no weak cryptography, commands, network calls, or prompt injection. No additional semantic threat evidence was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 3 describes loading common code patterns and abstractions. No cryptographic algorithm, implementation, or security-sensitive operation appears on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain frontmatter description text. There is no code or cryptographic context to support the static weak algorithm finding.

low_findings.title: False Positive: General Pattern Description Flagged
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 8 states that the skill provides common project patterns. This is documentation prose and does not reference cryptography or unsafe primitives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is a generic sentence about project patterns. No executable behavior or weak cryptographic algorithm is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: API Design Category Flagged
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 25 lists API design patterns as a category. The line is a topic label and does not contain cryptographic logic or dangerous behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a bullet item in a documentation list. It has no algorithm name, implementation, or security-sensitive operation.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when new code ignores established project conventions. This skill loads pattern guidance for features, components, APIs, state, and errors.
actual_capabilities: Loads common project code patterns for implementation work.
Guides new feature work toward existing abstractions.
Supports component creation with project conventions in mind.
Covers data access, API design, component, state, and error handling patterns.
Points the assistant to a project pattern reference file.
limitations: It depends on the referenced pattern file being available in the project.
It does not generate or validate code by itself.
It does not include framework-specific rules in SKILL.md.
It provides guidance, not an automated architecture review.

use_cases.title: Implement Consistent Features
use_cases.description: Use the skill before adding a feature so new code follows existing project structure.

use_cases.title: Create Project Components
use_cases.description: Use the skill when building components that should match established component patterns.

use_cases.title: Review Design Direction
use_cases.description: Use the skill while deciding data access, API, state, or error handling approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Load Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the patterns skill before implementing this change. Summarize the relevant project conventions first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use before a small implementation task

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the patterns skill to plan this feature. Identify the data access, API, component, state, and error handling patterns that apply.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a feature before writing code

prompt_templates.title: Compare Approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the patterns skill to compare two implementation approaches. Recommend the approach that best matches the existing project abstractions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Implementation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the patterns skill to review this implementation plan. Flag any places that conflict with project patterns and suggest corrections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before a larger code change

output_examples.input: Use the patterns skill before adding a settings panel.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies relevant component and state patterns, then proposes an implementation path aligned with the project conventions.

output_examples.input: Review this API design against the project patterns.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares the proposed API shape with the project guidance and lists concrete alignment gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan error handling for a new data access flow.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes the expected data access and error handling patterns before recommending a consistent flow.

best_practices: Load the skill before design decisions, not after code is complete.
Ask for the relevant pattern categories before requesting implementation.
Use the guidance together with direct inspection of nearby project code.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for tests or code review.
Do not assume patterns apply when the referenced project file is missing.
Do not force a pattern when local code clearly uses a different convention.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps assistants follow project code patterns when implementing features, components, APIs, state logic, and error handling.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. SKILL.md only provides guidance and references a pattern file for reading.

faq.question: Is this useful for new projects?
faq.answer: It is most useful when the project already has documented patterns or existing conventions.

faq.question: Can it replace architecture review?
faq.answer: No. It supports consistency checks, but human review is still needed for important design decisions.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide in a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the feature goal, affected files, and which pattern category you want the assistant to consider.
